title: vault/doppler/gcp-sm: HTTP error handling duplicated across get() and put()
description: What is awkward: vault, doppler, and gcp-sm each contain essentially the same status-code dispatch block (is_server_error → Unavailable, 404 → NotFound, other 4xx → Backend) copy-pasted in both get() and put(). In vault alone this pattern appears twice (once in get, once in put). Suggested rewrite: extract a per-backend helper fn map_http_error(status, detail, backend_name) -> SecretError that codifies the classification once. This reduces the surface area for inconsistencies (e.g. doppler's get() explicitly handles 401/403 as permanent while put() treats all 4xx as permanent — the policy divergence is invisible in the current structure). Not a functional bug today but will grow as more backends are added.
